title: Write decimals and fractions greater than 1 shown on grids
description: Practice writing decimal numbers shown in grid diagrams.
topic_path: Write decimals and fractions greater than 1 shown on grids

assessment_items.question_content:
Each big square below represents one whole.

![A big square with 10 of the 10 equal pieces shaded](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/580592d0ef7cf03a1d1834501615e5986f72508b)![A big square with 2 of the 10 equal pieces shaded](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b70c7547598f6a7a0dc443ac21f7829c1314f6ce)

**Express the shaded area as both a mixed number and a decimal.**

**Fraction:** [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
 
**Decimal:** [[☃ numeric-input 4]]
hints: Each big square represents one whole and has $10$ equal pieces, or *tenths*.

![A big square with 10 of the 10 equal pieces shaded, labeled 10 tenths](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/72616ad9528e4e3937617429ca9daeed690e46e1) $+$ ![A big square with 2 of the 10 equal pieces shaded, labeled 2 tenths](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a10745edaf680550b9300f2b4bdf6743715ba71a)

There are $\greenD{10\text{ tenths}}+\greenD{2 \text{ tenths}}$ shaded. We can write that as a fraction as $\greenD{\dfrac{12}{10}}$ or as $\greenD{1\dfrac{2}{10}}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can also show the same area using ones and tenths.

![A big square labeled 1 one](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5f07accd02e047a4d3404f420b4f03ecf115bf18) $+$ ![A big square with 2 of the 10 equal pieces shaded, labeled 2 tenths](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a10745edaf680550b9300f2b4bdf6743715ba71a)

We can use a place value chart to help us write $\purpleD{1 \text{ one}}+\greenD{2 \text{ tenths}} $ as a decimal.

Tens |Ones | $.$ | Tenths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: 
|$\purpleD{1}$|$.$  | $\greenD{2}$
The shaded area represents the fraction $\dfrac{12}{10}$ and the decimal $1.2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each big square below represents one whole.

![A big square with 10 of the 10 equal pieces shaded](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/46bb4099ba850bd436d9c781f0affc7c1de9b5e7)![A big square with 10 of the 10 equal pieces shaded](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/46bb4099ba850bd436d9c781f0affc7c1de9b5e7)![A big square with 10 of the 10 equal pieces shaded](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/46bb4099ba850bd436d9c781f0affc7c1de9b5e7)![A big square with 3 of the 10 equal pieces shaded](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0ebaa1d3c01143c5850213668b480395b62e2add)

**Express the shaded area as both a mixed number and a decimal.**

**Fraction:** [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
 
**Decimal:** [[☃ numeric-input 4]]
hints: Each big square represents one whole and has $10$ equal pieces, or *tenths*.

![A big square with 10 of the 10 equal pieces shaded, labeled 10 tenths](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/72616ad9528e4e3937617429ca9daeed690e46e1) $+$ ![A big square with 10 of the 10 equal pieces shaded, labeled 10 tenths](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/72616ad9528e4e3937617429ca9daeed690e46e1) $+$ ![A big square with 10 of the 10 equal pieces shaded, labeled 10 tenths](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/72616ad9528e4e3937617429ca9daeed690e46e1) $+$ ![A big square with 3 of the 10 equal pieces shaded, labeled 3 tenths](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a89f6622c4b479357b342f7cefca1fdaddefd687)

There are $\greenD{10\text{ tenths}}+\greenD{10\text{ tenths}}+{}$ $\greenD{10\text{ tenths}}+\greenD{3 \text{ tenths}}$ shaded. We can write that as a fraction as $\greenD{\dfrac{33}{10}}$ or as $\greenD{3\dfrac{3}{10}}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can also show the same area using ones and tenths.

![A big square labeled 1 one](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5f07accd02e047a4d3404f420b4f03ecf115bf18) $+$ ![A big square labeled 1 one](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5f07accd02e047a4d3404f420b4f03ecf115bf18) $+$ ![A big square labeled 1 one](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5f07accd02e047a4d3404f420b4f03ecf115bf18) $+$ ![A big square with 3 of the 10 equal pieces shaded, labeled 3 tenths](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a89f6622c4b479357b342f7cefca1fdaddefd687)

We can use a place value chart to help us write $\purpleD{3 \text{ ones}}+\greenD{3 \text{ tenths}} $ as a decimal.

Tens |Ones | $.$ | Tenths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: 
|$\purpleD{3}$|$.$  | $\greenD{3}$
The shaded area represents the fraction $\dfrac{33}{10}$ and the decimal $3.3$.

We can also show the same area using ones and tenths.

There are $\greenD{10\text{ tenths}}+\greenD{10\text{ tenths}}+\greenD{4 \text{ tenths}}$ shaded. We can write that as a fraction as $\greenD{\dfrac{24}{10}}$ or as $\greenD{2\dfrac{4}{10}}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can also show the same area using ones and tenths.

![A big square labeled 1 one](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5f07accd02e047a4d3404f420b4f03ecf115bf18) $+$ ![A big square labeled 1 one](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5f07accd02e047a4d3404f420b4f03ecf115bf18) $+$ ![A big square with 4 of the 10 equal pieces shaded, labeled 4 tenths](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0dd34b7025c7d99af7adb62473c35d63e8c0464d)

The shaded area represents the fraction $\dfrac{24}{10}$ and the decimal $2.4$.

There are $\greenD{10\text{ tenths}}+\greenD{10\text{ tenths}}+\greenD{7 \text{ tenths}}$ shaded. We can write that as a fraction as $\greenD{\dfrac{27}{10}}$ or as $\greenD{2\dfrac{7}{10}}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can also show the same area using ones and tenths.

![A big square labeled 1 one](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5f07accd02e047a4d3404f420b4f03ecf115bf18) $+$ ![A big square labeled 1 one](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5f07accd02e047a4d3404f420b4f03ecf115bf18) $+$ ![A big square with 7 of the 10 equal pieces shaded, labeled 7 tenths](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0f817873b5158c9986462763a359b3c40eae636e)

assessment_items.question_content:
Each big square below represents one whole.

![A big square with 100 of the 100 equal pieces shaded](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e65b969e686c45306bd089f9da1e863067adab86)![A big square with 5 of the 100 equal pieces shaded](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/36bda3f4973ca26af11224841ff40a901d121ab5)

**Express the shaded area as both a fraction and a decimal.**

**Fraction:** [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
 
**Decimal:** [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: Each big square represents one whole and has $100$ equal pieces, or *hundredths*.

![A big square with 100 of the 100 equal pieces shaded, labeled 100 hundredths](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a05629ecf9b26509b86548324fe3272f090d54d7) $+$ ![A big square with 5 of the 100 equal pieces shaded, labeled 5 hundredths](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/27e074e401dcdd34784e1602e740d1d075fa1cbe)

There are $\greenD{100 \text{ hundredths}}$ $+$ $\greenD{5 \text{ hundredths}}$ shaded. We can write that as a fraction as $\greenD{\dfrac{105}{100}}$ or as $\greenD{1\dfrac{5}{100}}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can also show the same area using ones, tenths, and hundredths.

![A big square labeled 1 one](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2e74b818b3477dfd538a4fcb23f1267f69f0e032) $+$ ![A big square with 5 of the 100 equal pieces shaded, labeled 5 hundredths](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/621c82b7456b503645f4f969719b719ca9c98437)

We can use a place value chart to help us write $\maroonD{1 \text{ one}}+\greenD{5 \text{ hundredths}}$ as a decimal.

Tens |Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$\maroonD{1}$|$.$  | $0$ | $\greenD{5}$
The shaded area represents the fraction $\dfrac{105}{100}$ and the decimal $1.05$.

Each big square below represents one whole.

hints: Each big square represents one whole and has $100$ equal pieces, or *hundredths*.

There are $\greenD{100 \text{ hundredths}+19 \text{ hundredths}}$ shaded. We can write that as a fraction as $\greenD{\dfrac{119}{100}}$ or as $\greenD{1\dfrac{19}{100}}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can also show the same area using ones, tenths, and hundredths.

![A big square labeled 1 one](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ca576622ad3babf56267ae6364bf7b204b76a380) $+$ ![A big square with 1 of the 10 equal pieces shaded, labeled 1 tenth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4201372aa59fc0002bb190ff336eb34429d348f3) $+$ ![A big square with 9 of the 100 equal pieces shaded, labeled 9 hundredths](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/30946b93325c21951d34c5fa6e4c4e80523bc39b)

We can use a place value chart to help us write $\maroonD{1 \text{ one}}+\purpleD{1 \text{ tenth}} + \greenD{9 \text{ hundredths}}$ as a decimal.

Tens |Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$\maroonD{1}$|$.$  | $\purpleD{1}$ | $\greenD{9}$
The shaded area represents the fraction $\dfrac{119}{100}$ and the decimal $1.19$.

The shaded area represents the fraction $\dfrac{203}{100}$ and the decimal $2.03$.

There are $\blueD{11 \text{ tenths}}$ shaded.

![Two equal-sized squares, the first square divided into ten equal-sized rows all shaded. Below the square is the label 10. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7995bc280778a4e84623f07db6edec71d3169455) $+$![A second square is also divided into ten equal-sized rows. The top row shaded. Below the square is the label 2. A plus sign is between the 2 squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/899a61124051c6f0e8800b7a1b5192e8818f3d74)

We can also express $\blueD{11 \text{ tenths}}$ as $\maroonD{1 \text{ one}}$ and $\blueD{1 \text{ tenth}}$:


[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ image 2]]

$\blueD{11 \text{ tenths}}$ as $\maroonD{1 \text{ one}}$ and $\blueD{1 \text{ tenth}}$ as a decimal.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$\maroonD{1}$|$.$  | $\blue1$ |

The shaded area represents the following:

* $11$ tenths

* $1.1$
